---
title: "Skills"
description: "Dissertation workflow: psychophysics, pupillometry QC, psychometric function modeling, mixed models, hierarchical Bayesian Wiener DDM (brms/Stan/CmdStan), MATLAB/PsychToolbox, R, Quarto/LaTeX. Adjacent: web hand-vs-gaze task, PyMC LBA for verification RTs (XR preprint)."
format:
  html:
    output-file: index.html
---

This page is **how** the [**Research**](../research/research.qmd) program is implemented—**methods, workflow, and computation**—not a generic résumé. The **dissertation toolkit** fills the sections below; **XR / interaction** methods are **adjacent** at the end. [**Publications**](../publications/publications.qmd) · [**CV**](../cv.qmd)

---

## Core Research Methods

- **Psychophysics:** **Auditory and visual same–different discrimination** (and related tasks) under load; linking **physical effort** to sensitivity, bias, and dynamics.
- **Pupillometry:** **Preprocessing and quality control** (blinks, artifacts, valid samples); **window-specific validity**; **gap-aware** handling of missing samples.
- **Interpretation:** Pupil measures inform **arousal** and **effort** hypotheses; **behavior** and formal models remain primary for inference (aligned with [**Research**](../research/research.qmd)).

---

## Experimental Design and Data Collection

- **Handgrip dual-task paradigm:** **Concurrent physical effort** (graded isometric grip) with cognitive/perceptual tasks, including **same–different discrimination** and memory components as required.
- **Within-subject designs:** Factorial and repeated-measures layouts; **counterbalancing** of orders and conditions.
- **Preregistration:** Where used, analyses follow registered hypotheses, exclusions, and plans.
- **Lab tasks:** **MATLAB** and **PsychToolbox** for stimulus delivery and responses; calibration and exclusion rules documented in materials.

---

## Modeling and Statistical Inference

- **Psychometric function modeling:** Separate effects on perceptual **evidence** versus **criterion** under **physical effort** and load.
- **Mixed-effects models:** **LMMs** / **GLMMs** in **R** (**lme4**, **emmeans**) for repeated measures and individual differences ([**dual-task roadmap**](../projects/dual-task-case-study.qmd)).
- **Hierarchical Bayesian Wiener drift-diffusion model (DDM)—dissertation:** Trial-level decomposition (**drift rate**, **boundaries**, **non-decision time**); implemented with **brms** / **Stan** (**CmdStan**-class) workflows. Scope and caveats (**non-decision time** constrained by design; **pupil–parameter** links exploratory) are stated on [**Research**](../research/research.qmd).
- **Scrutiny:** Sensitivity analyses, alternative specifications, model comparison, interval- or equivalence-style reasoning where relevant.

**XR preprint only:** [**case study**](../projects/case_study_web.qmd) uses **hierarchical Bayesian LBA** in **PyMC** for **verification-phase** RTs after target entry—**not** the dissertation **Wiener DDM**, and **not** interchangeable with it.

---

## Programming and Research Computing

- **R:** **tidyverse**, **ggplot2**; mixed models; **brms** / **Stan** interfaces; **Quarto** for analyses and this site.
- **MATLAB:** **PsychToolbox**.
- **Git** / **GitHub:** Versioned scripts and reproducible layout.

---

## Scientific Communication and Reproducibility

- **Quarto** / **R Markdown** (legacy) for rebuildable reports.
- **LaTeX** for manuscripts; exploratory vs. confirmatory reporting kept distinct where both apply.
- **Traceability:** README notes, pinned dependencies where used, QC logs for **pupillometry** and behavioral pipelines.

---

## Additional / Adjacent Methods

**Hand vs. gaze interaction (portfolio / preprint)**—[**arXiv:2603.15991**](https://arxiv.org/abs/2603.15991), [case study](../projects/case_study_web.qmd). **Adjacent** to the dissertation; adds **HCI / XR-relevant** breadth:

- **Web task:** **React** / **TypeScript**; **remote** sessions; **display calibration** and session checks across devices.
- **Design:** **ISO 9241-9**-style multidirectional tapping; **Fitts** difficulty and **throughput**; **Williams** block counterbalancing; **NASA-TLX** workload.
- **Gaze proxy:** **Physiologically informed gaze simulation** (latency, jitter, saccadic suppression) for **gaze** vs. **hand** comparison without lab eye-tracking.
- **Policy-triggered adaptive UI:** **Declutter** (gaze) and **width inflation** (hand) implemented and logged. In the analyzed data, **only declutter executed** and was **evaluable** (modest **timeout** reduction; **slips** still dominated **gaze** errors). **Hand width inflation was not evaluable**—targets did not scale in the UI (**integration bug**); that policy is **not** validated here.
- **LBA fits:** **Python** / **PyMC** for verification-phase RTs only—see **Modeling** note above.

**Other:** [Surgeon dashboard](../projects/surgeon-performance-predict2.qmd) (R Shiny, XGBoost); [EEG thesis](../projects/memory-and-meditation.qmd); imaging collaborations—[**Publications**](../publications/publications.qmd); SST—[**About**](../about/about.qmd).

---

## Languages

Persian (native), English (professional), French (basic).
